title: How to install the latest version 1.6.1 of skopeo on CentOS7/SLES15/RHEL8 Operating systems?
body:
How to install the latest version of skopeo on CentOS7/SLES15/RHEL8 Operating systems?

CentOS7: yum install -y skopeo
In this case skopeo version 0.1.40 is installed

SLES:  zypper in -y skopeo
In this case, skopeo version 0.1.41 is installed

How do I install skopeo version 1.6.1 on these Operating Systems?

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

Stable distributions generally don’t update to latest versions of software. Compare also https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/1277 .

Assuming a new enough Go tooling, you can build Skopeo from source, using something like https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/main/install.md#building-without-a-container , or perhaps make your own packages.

@mtrmac I want to use the skopeo login and skopeo logout feature in my project which is only available in v1.0.0 and above. Any possibility that any skopeo package version between v1.0.0 and v1.6.1 will be made available for RHEL and SLES OS distriution?

What RHEL and SLES provide is decided by RHEL and SLES.

As for the upstream Skopeo project providing third-party packages for these distributions, my reading of #1277 is that we are no longer doing that for these distributions; I’ll let @lsm5 confirm.
An alternative may be using the container images we provide on `quay.io/skopeo/stable`.  The images includes skopeo and can be used as `podman run quay.io/skopeo/stable $command`.
In general, different Linux distributions are not binary-compatible, at the very least due to version differences. Some specific pairs may be.
